notes: DOCUMENTED TARGET — the codex recipe is marked applicable:false (see scenarios.json by_adapter.codex.notes): a live codex session cannot be deterministically driven to emit a >64 MB (or even >2 MB) single JSONL line, so there is no recording. This is shared-tailer robustness, not a codex behavior. irrlicht's tailer (core/pkg/tailer/tailer.go) uses bufio.NewReaderSize + readLineCapped with a 64 MB cap (issue #270 regression hardening); lines below 64 MB parse normally and lines above are logged-and-skipped past without stalling. The authoritative coverage is the agent-agnostic unit test core/pkg/tailer/large_line_test.go (codex's JSONLineParser runs on the same tailer). Spec asserts only the state arc: the absence of a parser stall is INFERRED from the arc completing — if the tailer wedged on the long line, working→ready would never fire.

text: Oversized assistant line is processed (or skipped past, under the 64 MB cap); terminal stop lands; working → ready. The completion of this transition is the spec's parser-resilience proof.
